facts.statement: Pfizer is classified under SIC code 'Pharmaceutical Preparations' and trades on the NYSE under the ticker PFE.
Pfizer reported total revenues of $62.6 billion for full-year 2025, compared with $63.6 billion the previous year, reflecting a year-over-year operational decline of 2%.
Excluding contributions from COVID-19 products, Pfizer's revenues grew 6% operationally in 2025.
Pfizer reported net income of $2,119,000,000 for FY2025.
Pfizer generated operating cash flow of $8,700,000,000 for FY2025.
As of FY2025, Pfizer had stockholders' equity of $88,203,000,000, long-term debt of $57,405,000,000, and cash and equivalents of $1,043,000,000.

In 2025, Pfizer returned $9.8 billion to shareholders via its quarterly dividend and invested $10.4 billion in internal R&D and approximately $8.8 billion in business development transactions, primarily reflecting the Metsera acquisition and the 3SBio licensing deal.

Pfizer's capital allocation strategy prioritizes maintaining and, over the long term, growing its dividend, reinvesting in the business, and the potential for share repurchases after de-leveraging its balance sheet.

Pfizer's cost realignment program is expected to deliver total net cost savings of approximately $5.7 billion by the end of 2026, and the first phase of its manufacturing optimization program is on track to deliver approximately $1.5 billion in net cost savings by the end of 2027.
Albert Bourla serves as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Pfizer, and David M. Denton serves as Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President.

Jennifer B. Damico serves as Senior Vice President and Controller (Principal Accounting Officer), and Margaret M. Madden serves as Senior Vice President and Corporate Secretary, Chief Governance Counsel.

Pfizer's Board of Directors includes Ronald E. Blaylock, Mortimer J. Buckley, Susan Desmond-Hellmann, Joseph J. Echevarria, Scott Gottlieb, Susan Hockfield, Dan R. Littman, Shantanu Narayen, Suzanne Nora Johnson, James Quincey, James C. Smith, and Cyrus Taraporevala.
Dr. Susan Hockfield will retire from the Pfizer Board and will not stand for re-election in 2026.

Pfizer completed the acquisition of Seagen Inc. on December 14, 2023, with associated restructuring, integration, and acquisition-related costs, as well as fair value adjustments to inventory and amortization expense.
Pfizer acquired Metsera, Inc. in 2025, reinvigorating its presence in obesity with an ultra-long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist and an expansive Phase 3 program.
Pfizer entered into in-licensing agreements with 3SBio Inc. and YaoPharma Co., Ltd. in 2025 as part of its strategic deals to position the company for future growth.

Pfizer has 13 manufacturing and distribution facilities in the United States, comprising one of the largest U.S. internal pharmaceutical manufacturing networks, with nearly 40% of colleagues working in the global manufacturing and supply network producing about 45 billion doses of more than 500 medicines and vaccines each year for approximately 200 countries and territories.

Pfizer's 10-K references multiple product and brand members including Tukysa, Zavzpret, Medrol, Prevnar 13, disitamab vedotin, osivelotor, etrasimod (Velsipity), and B7H4 felmetatug vedotin, as well as U.S. Sterile Injectable and Hospital Products.
Pfizer has a manufacturing and supply agreement with Viatris and maintains a relationship with ViiV Healthcare Limited, a collaborative arrangement in HIV.

Pfizer reached a voluntary agreement with the U.S. Government in 2025 supporting efforts to lower prescription drug costs and align prices with those in other developed countries, providing greater clarity on pricing and a three-year grace period from certain U.S. tariffs.

Pfizer sold its equity method investment in Haleon through public stock offerings and private placements during 2024 and 2025, with transactions occurring in March 2024, October 2024, and throughout 2025.

Pfizer has non-recourse financing arrangements with Blackstone (April 2023 and March 2025) and Abingworth (September 2025) involving clinical trial agreement terms, approval-based payment periods, and net sales and royalty agreement terms.

Pfizer's independent registered public accounting firm is KPMG LLP, New York, NY, Auditor Firm ID: 185, and management assessed the company's internal control over financial reporting as effective as of December 31, 2025.

Pfizer's Accord for a Healthier World initiative, launched in 2022 to provide its entire portfolio of medicines and vaccines to 45 lower-income countries on a not-for-profit basis, reached nearly 800,000 patients in 2025, a fourfold increase from the previous year.
